context: Tests whether the agent implements the password reset flow with correct token generation (randomBytes + SHA-256 hash stored), 1-hour expiry, email enumeration prevention, and full session/token invalidation after a successful reset.

checklist.name: Email enumeration prevention
checklist.description: The forgot-password endpoint returns a success-like message even when no account exists for the given email — does NOT reveal whether an account exists (e.g., always returns 'If an account exists, a reset link has been sent' or equivalent)

checklist.name: crypto.randomBytes token
checklist.description: Uses Node.js built-in 'crypto' module's randomBytes(32) to generate the password reset token (not Math.random, uuid, or other methods)

checklist.name: Token as hex string
checklist.description: The raw reset token is converted to a hex string with .toString('hex')

checklist.name: SHA-256 hash stored
checklist.description: Only the SHA-256 hash of the token (crypto.createHash('sha256')...) is stored in the database — the raw token is NOT persisted

checklist.name: 1-hour token expiry
checklist.description: The reset token expiry is set to 1 hour from creation (Date.now() + 60 * 60 * 1000 or equivalent 3600 second offset)

checklist.name: Expired token check
checklist.description: The reset-password endpoint validates that the token has not expired before allowing a password change

checklist.name: Invalidate reset token after use
checklist.description: After a successful password reset, the used password reset record/token is deleted or invalidated so it cannot be reused

checklist.name: Invalidate all sessions
checklist.description: After a successful password reset, all existing sessions for that customer are invalidated/deleted (not just the current one)

checklist.name: bcrypt for new password
checklist.description: The new password is hashed with bcrypt at cost factor 12 before being stored

checklist.name: Hash lookup (not raw)
checklist.description: When verifying the reset token, the submitted token is hashed first and then compared against the stored hash — does NOT compare the raw token directly against stored values
